title: Sesame Tofu & Broccoli
description: Crispy baked tofu is tossed with a sweet and savory, chili-spiked sesame sauce and crisp-tender broccoli for a delicious vegetarian weeknight meal.

ingredients.item: scallions, thinly sliced, green and white parts separated
piece fresh ginger, peeled and grated
kosher salt, plus more to taste

instructions: Wrap tofu block in paper towels or a clean kitchen towel and weight with a heavy skillet or can for 30 to 45 minutes to press out excess moisture.

Preheat oven to 425°F. In a bowl, whisk together grated ginger, garlic, white scallion parts, vegetable broth, soy sauce, maple syrup, chili crisp, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and 1 tbsp cornstarch until smooth.

Break pressed tofu into rough 1-inch pieces in a bowl. Toss gently with 1/2 tsp salt and 1 tbsp neutral oil. Add remaining 1/3 cup cornstarch and toss until evenly coated. Spread onto a parchment-lined baking sheet and drizzle with 2 tbsp neutral oil.
Bake tofu for about 25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until crispy and golden brown.

Heat 1 tbsp neutral oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add broccoli florets, season with salt, and stir-fry for 4 to 5 minutes until crisp-tender and slightly charred. Remove broccoli from skillet and set aside.

Reduce skillet heat to medium, re-whisk sauce, and pour into skillet. Simmer for about 4 minutes until thickened. Return broccoli and baked tofu to skillet, tossing to coat in sauce. Garnish with green scallion parts and sesame seeds, and serve over steamed white rice.
